Unfortunately, this is not the first time I have faced such issues, and it is becoming increasingly concerning, especially from a well-established and reputed brand like yours.

The pizza delivered was extremely dry and lacked the freshness and standard quality that customers associate with Domino’s. In particular, the Cheese Volcano Pizza had a noticeably insufficient amount of cheese, which completely defeats the core appeal of that product. The texture was dry, and the overall taste did not meet the expected standards.

Additionally, another item in the same order was also below quality expectations in terms of taste and preparation. When customers choose a trusted brand like Domino’s, they do so with confidence in consistent quality. Repeated experiences of substandard food are disappointing and reflect poorly on quality control and service standards.

I request that this matter be looked into seriously. Kindly review the quality checks at the outlet responsible for this order and ensure that proper preparation standards are followed consistently. I would also appreciate appropriate compensation or corrective action for the inconvenience caused.

I look forward to your prompt response and hope to see an improvement in future orders.

Achari Tandoori Momo - 8pc - ₹140 ; 4pc - ₹110

The taste was perfect. The filling was very tasty. It was cooked properly from the outside. Loved the flavours.
And the green chutney was complimenting the taste so well. They serve one of the best momo chutneys out there.

They also serve a dip at ₹10. But personally I would prefer the dip for fried momos. With tandoori momos chutney are the best.

Chicken Cheese burst momo - 8pc - ₹180 ;4pc - ₹150
The taste was good. The outside was cooked pretty well and the fillings were tasty. The cheese in between was giving a nice texture.
I would recommend the mayo dip with it, it alleviates the taste.
But it could have more cheese in it. The cheese seemed less to be called cheese burst momo.

Chicken Afghani momos - 8pc - ₹140 ; 4pc - ₹110
It was also good. Properly cooked and tasty filling. The chutney was complementing the taste.But they could still work a bit on their afghani flavour.

Coming to an conclusion, this place serves one of the best momos in town ( and the best chutneys ). It's better to order full plate only as the half plates are priced quite unreasonably here.
The owner is very friendly. I say this as a loyal, financially dedicated customer whom you will see here every third day: we need to protect this haven. For many of us, it’s a source of genuine peace and warmth. If its integrity is compromised, I will have wasted half a year's wages on a temporary fix. Peace out!♥️🤌🏼","text_translated":"","reply_text_original":"Thank you!","reply_language":"en","reply_posted_at_unix_micros":1762665968000000,"reply_updated_at_unix_micros":1762665968000000,"published_at":"2025-11-08T17:47:51.498369Z"}]

from this restaurant on Zomato and was completely blown away with the taste of their pizza. So, thought of reaching directly out to the restaurant to order the same pizza that would otherwise cost less. But to my disappointment, i found the toppings were less every so slightly. And it didn’t melt into my mouth as it did when first ordered from Zomato. A bit disappointed.

So, I think the restaurant still is awesome with quality food but given the premium price they are charging on their menu, they have work cut out to popular brands like PizzaHut and Dominos maintaining the consistency of quality and quantity of their food.

Service is below average since they don’t have any staff to cater to the customers. The space too is not big enough to accommodate more than 4-5 people in comfort. It’s cramped. So, gave three star for “space”. Average.

Atmosphere and ambience too was somewhat-average. There was no soothing music playing. No plants, greenery or arts on the wall that gives a soothing appeal. Some decorative rope lights and light strips etc could also lift the mood of the customer. They were missing too. So… they need to work on interior of the shop given the kind of service they offer. Ambience and atmosphere matters a lot. Therefore average rating for “Atmosphere” too.

Lastly, it would be better if they could offer service on a larger radius as to not completely depend upon Zomato and Swiggy. One downer of both of these platforms is that they have an exorbitantly high service charge. I personally, don’t like ordering food from Zomato and Swiggy often. Once I get to know a good restaurant, I try to order food directly from them and save money on the same order.

Tried FFC for takeaway and honestly, it was a fantastic experience from start to finish.

The peri peri fries were super crispy and packed with spice — easily a 5/5 on spice 🌶️. Crispiness was on point (4.5/5). Quantity was decent, though I wouldn’t mind a little more next time.

Now coming to the star of the show — the spicy paneer pizza 🍕🔥
The paneer was fresh and perfectly cooked (5/5), spice level was bold and satisfying (5/5), and the cheese… wow — 6/5 🤌🧀. The base was soft, airy, and perfectly baked, and the amount of toppings was very generous. Every bite tasted fresh and well-balanced.

Special mention to the owner, who was extremely professional and very humble — that always makes the experience better. The place itself was clean, well-maintained, and had a great ambience (5/5 for both).

Overall, this place is extremely value for money and clearly focuses on freshness and quality.
Will definitely be coming back and recommending it to fellow food lovers!

[{"Name":"Abhay Kumar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVKOyEI9MH5g0_p9nYI7XWZRYy72ZJ9ch8lxs1x4kJAIM_Ex1Nu=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"4½ ⭐
I recently visited the restaurant with my brothers. It's a nice and clean place. The staff were polite, friendly, and helped us well.

We asked for food without garlic and onion, and they gave us exactly what we wanted. The food was tasty.

One small issue – the hand dryer in the bathroom was not working (I have attached video for that).

Since it’s a family restaurant, I think people should keep the volume low when playing videos on their phones. During our visit, some parents gave their kids phones, and the videos were playing at full volume, which was quite irritating.

I would also suggest to the owner or manager if possible, please add some light background music in the restaurant. It will make the place feel even better and help cover up other noise.

I recently visited the restaurant with my brothers. It's a nice and clean place. The staff were polite, friendly, and helped us well.

We asked for food without garlic and onion, and they gave us exactly what we wanted. The food was tasty.

One small issue – the hand dryer in the bathroom was not working (I have attached video for that).

Since it’s a family restaurant, I think people should keep the volume low when playing videos on their phones. During our visit, some parents gave their kids phones, and the videos were playing at full volume, which was quite irritating.

I would also suggest to the owner or manager if possible, please add some light background music in the restaurant. It will make the place feel even better and help cover up other noise.

Overall, a great place for families. user_reviews:
[{"Name":"KETAN SHEKHAR","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX0rDeKo_pyv-V0idzJ1-dk849nxEIIyHyBCGUjuox2EI0nJ1o=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The Ruin House, Ranchi – A Rustic Escape with Urban Vibes

The Ruin House is one of the most visually captivating cafés in Ranchi, blending rustic architecture with a modern, bohemian charm. The first thing that strikes you is the ambience—exposed brick walls, vintage décor, open-air seating, warm lighting, and a ruin-style theme that feels artistic yet comfortable. Evenings often feature live music or DJ nights, making it a perfect place to unwind with friends or plan a memorable date.

The menu offers a mix of North Indian, Continental, Chinese, and café-style dishes. Starters like tandoori platters, pasta, and sizzlers stand out, and the desserts—especially brownies and lava cakes—are crowd favourites. Presentation is appealing, and portion sizes are decent. While the food is generally good, consistency can vary depending on rush hours, and service may feel slightly slow during peak times. However, the staff is polite and makes an effort to guide you with recommendations.

What I appreciate most is the overall experience. It’s more than just a place to eat—it's an aesthetic space to relax, click pictures, enjoy music, and soak in a unique atmosphere. Pricing is moderate to slightly premium, but the ambience and vibe make it worth it.

✅ Best for: Evenings out, celebrations, dates, hangouts
✅ Highlights: Ambience, décor, live music

Ruin House, Ranchi – A Rustic Escape with Urban Vibes

The Ruin House is one of the most visually captivating cafés in Ranchi, blending rustic architecture with a modern, bohemian charm. The first thing that strikes you is the ambience—exposed brick walls, vintage décor, open-air seating, warm lighting, and a ruin-style theme that feels artistic yet comfortable. Evenings often feature live music or DJ nights, making it a perfect place to unwind with friends or plan a memorable date.

The menu offers a mix of North Indian, Continental, Chinese, and café-style dishes. Starters like tandoori platters, pasta, and sizzlers stand out, and the desserts—especially brownies and lava cakes—are crowd favourites. Presentation is appealing, and portion sizes are decent. While the food is generally good, consistency can vary depending on rush hours, and service may feel slightly slow during peak times. However, the staff is polite and makes an effort to guide you with recommendations.

What I appreciate most is the overall experience. It’s more than just a place to eat—it's an aesthetic space to relax, click pictures, enjoy music, and soak in a unique atmosphere. Pricing is moderate to slightly premium, but the ambience and vibe make it worth it.

✅ Best for: Evenings out, celebrations, dates, hangouts
✅ Highlights: Ambience, décor, live music

My wife and I were charged Rs 2000 as cover charges on the pretext that since we had booked through the EasyDiner app, we would be seated downstairs where there was live music and a live cricket match. We were clearly told that if we did not pay the cover charge, we would be sent upstairs where there would be no live entertainment. Trusting this, we paid the amount at the entrance.

However, once we went inside, we were forcefully made to sit upstairs, despite multiple tables being clearly available downstairs. When we questioned this, the staff had no proper explanation and simply avoided addressing the issue. This felt misleading and unfair, especially after paying a premium cover charge for a specific experience that was never delivered.

To make matters worse, no one came to take our order for a long time. We had to repeatedly look for staff, and even then, the service was slow, unorganized, and careless. There was a complete lack of coordination among the staff, and several small mishaps kept happening throughout our visit, making the overall environment feel chaotic rather than enjoyable.

A place that presents itself as a high-end club and dining experience should value transparency, customer comfort, and professional service. Unfortunately, The Ruin House failed on all three fronts during our visit.

I would strongly suggest management look into staff training, customer handling, and ethical practices regarding cover charges and seating policies. When we questioned this, the staff had no proper explanation and simply avoided addressing the issue. This felt misleading and unfair, especially after paying a premium cover charge for a specific experience that was never delivered.

To make matters worse, no one came to take our order for a long time. We had to repeatedly look for staff, and even then, the service was slow, unorganized, and careless. There was a complete lack of coordination among the staff, and several small mishaps kept happening throughout our visit, making the overall environment feel chaotic rather than enjoyable.

From the moment the box was opened, the aroma of freshly baked dough and melted cheese filled the room, setting high expectations that were thoroughly met.
​The visual appeal of the pizza was the first thing that caught my eye. It arrived perfectly baked to a golden-brown hue, generously dotted with succulent cubes of marinated paneer and vibrant, fresh green capsicum. Everything rested on a thick, gooey bed of perfectly melted mozzarella cheese that had those beautiful charred spots we all love.
​Talking about the taste and texture, each bite delivered a satisfying contrast. The paneer was incredibly soft, fresh, and mildly creamy, having absorbed the aromatic Indian spices perfectly. In contrast, the capsicum retained its signature crunch and added a subtle, refreshing sweetness that cut through the richness of the cheese.
​The foundation of the pizza—the crust and the sauce—was equally impressive. The tomato sauce had a lovely tanginess with a hint of herbs, providing a zesty base. The crust struck the perfect balance between being crispy on the outside and soft and chewy on the inside, acting as a sturdy yet delicious vessel for the toppings.
​Overall, this pizza is a fantastic fusion, expertly balancing traditional Indian flavors with a classic Italian favorite. It is comfort food at its finest and a must-try for anyone who enjoys a 'desi' twist on their pizza. Whether you are a vegetarian or just a pizza lover, this dish will not disappoint.
